intro: Eugenia guanabarina, also known as Guanabara guava, is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the Myrtaceae family. The health benefits of Eugenia guanabarina include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the treatment of various diseases, such as diabetes and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Eugenia guanabarina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Eugenia guanabarina is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
